domain: mathematics / dynamical systems (Lyapunov exponent spectrum, Oseledets decomposition)
specific_mechanism: The Lyapunov exponent spectrum quantifies, for a dynamical system, the exponential rates at which infinitesimally close trajectories separate along each direction of the tangent bundle; the Oseledets decomposition splits the tangent space into invariant subspaces each carrying one exponent; the signs of the spectrum classify the system (all negative: contracting; mixed: chaotic; positive: expanding)
llm_application: For a population of LLM agents collaborating on a task, treat the joint state-evolution as a dynamical system; compute the empirical Lyapunov exponent spectrum of the inter-agent communication trajectory; use the spectrum sign pattern to diagnose whether collaboration is contracting (consensus), expanding (divergence cascade), or chaotic (productive disagreement). The Oseledets-style decomposition identifies which agent pairs drive each Lyapunov direction.
novelty_claim: Existing multi-agent diagnostic uses aggregate metrics (consensus error, task completion). No published 2024-2026 LLM/AI multi-agent work computes the Lyapunov exponent spectrum + Oseledets decomposition of inter-agent communication trajectories as the diagnostic.
